[JS Weird World] No.57 ES6 and Classes
This chapter is all about ES6 (JavaScript EcmaScript 2015, aka EcmaScript 6). This time we're looking at yet another way to create objects and set up prototypes.

keyword is hugely popular in a bunch of other languages. You use it to define what an object's methods and properties should do. But like I mentioned before, JavaScript itself doesn't actually have classes.
So what does a class look like?
language-js shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
Basically it's the same as writing a function constructor — there's really no big difference in how you do it.
But here's the thing you need to watch out for: JavaScript's
is different from other languages. What you build with
is really just creating an object.
How do you set up the prototype?

This chapter covers ES6 (JavaScript EcmaScript 2015, aka EcmaScript 6) — specifically another way to create objects and set up prototypes. The class keyword is super popular in a lot of other languages, where you use it to define an object's methods and properties. But as we covered earlier, JavaScript doesn't actually have real classes. Let's see what ES6 classes really are under the hood.
